edible_parts: Young spring shoots (cooked, with caution); berries not eaten.
other_uses: Fiercely spiny large shrub/small tree with huge compound leaves and late-summer berry heads.
prep: Very young shoots have been cooked like the related Aralia spikenard, but the plant is heavily armed and mature parts are mildly toxic — treat as marginal.
soil: Rich, moist woodland soils and edges.
risks: Sharp prickles on stems; berries mildly toxic raw.
buy: Native-plant nurseries and native seed suppliers.
